steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The text mentions 'demographic characteristics' and 'neighborhood type' as factors 'some studies emphasize,' then pivots with 'but walking decisions are made in complex contexts in which multiple conditions and needs inform individuals' choices.' The correct answer must capture both parts: these factors matter (per the studies), but they're only part of a larger picture.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — They illustrate factors that researchers believe people consider when making walking decisions in most contexts but that the text argues are unique to walking decisions made by people in Zurich.: The text never says these factors are unique to Zurich — it uses Zurich only to introduce the walkability problem. ✗
  • Choice B — They are examples of factors that studies suggest are important in people's decision-making about walking but that the text claims most people rarely consider when making walking decisions.: The text never claims people don't think about these factors; it argues that research on them is incomplete, not that people ignore them. ✗
  • Choice C — They represent factors that have been identified as important influences on walking decisions but that the text suggests are merely some of the many factors that may contribute to people's decision-making about walking.: Matches both halves — 'some studies emphasize' them (identified as important), but the 'but' clause says walking decisions involve 'multiple conditions and needs' (merely some of many). ✓
  • Choice D — They identify factors that Andrade argues have been overemphasized in studies of decision-making about walking but that the text asserts are relevant to most people's walking decisions.: Andrade's point is that our understanding is insufficiently robust because no single factor captures the full picture — she doesn't say any factor is overemphasized. ✗
C is correct.
